title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Spiral Review
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

Inspect the Terminal Evidence Record trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

content.text: For Terminal Evidence Record, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Terminal Evidence Record precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A screenshot of output proves the conclusion without context.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Shell Terminal Kernel evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Shell Terminal Kernel assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Terminal Evidence Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
